title: Misapprehension
content:
Out of my heart, one day, I wrote a song,
    With my heart's blood imbued,
    Instinct with passion, tremulously strong,
    With grief subdued;
    Breathing a fortitude
    Pain-bought.
    And one who claimed much love for what I wrought,
    Read and considered it,
    And spoke:
    "Ay, brother,--'t is well writ,
    But where's the joke?"
meaning:
This evocative piece by Paul Laurence Dunbar, titled "Misapprehension", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
